inputs.label: Original Price
inputs.businessContext: The initial price before any discount. Standard pricing practice per ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.1.5 (monitoring and measuring resources).

inputs.label: Discount Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Percentage reduction applied to original price. Common in retail and B2B pricing strategies; referenced in financial accounting standards (IFRS 15).

inputs.label: Quantity
inputs.businessContext: Number of items purchased. Volume discounts are typical in supply chain management (APICS/SCOR model).

inputs.label: Tax Rate
inputs.businessContext: Sales tax or VAT applicable. Based on local tax regulations; ISO 14000 may apply for environmental taxes.

inputs.label: Additional Discount
inputs.businessContext: Fixed additional discount (e.g., coupon). Common in promotional campaigns; referenced in marketing ROI analysis.

inputs.label: Currency
inputs.businessContext: Currency for monetary values. ISO 4217 currency codes standard.

rules: additionalDiscount must be <= originalPrice * quantity * (1 - discountPercent/100) (cannot exceed discounted subtotal)

thresholds.discountPercent > 50: High discount may indicate clearance or loss leader; check profitability.
thresholds.taxRate > 20: High tax jurisdiction; consider tax optimization strategies.
thresholds.quantity > 1000: Bulk order; verify logistics capacity per Lean principles.

hiddenLossDrivers: discountPercent > 50 ? 'High discount rate may erode margin' : ''
taxRate > 20 ? 'High tax rate reduces net revenue' : ''
suggestedActions: discountPercent > 30 ? 'Consider reducing discount to maintain profitability' : ''
quantity > 500 ? 'Evaluate bulk shipping costs and storage' : ''

premiumFeatures: Trend analysis of discount usage over time
Detailed report with charts and recommendations
